column1: Category 2 (Tier-0) - Rice Growth & Water-Use Forcing Functions

Drivers for M_k(t) (compartment masses) and Q_TP(t) (transpiration stream): biomass partitioning, harvest index, root:shoot, LAI, ET/Kc, growth duration. These are PFAS-INDEPENDENT - best generated by a calibrated crop model; values below are anchors/sanity checks.

column2: Assimilate partitioning fractions (leaves FLV, stems FST, panicle FSO, roots FRT)
column3: DVS-dependent tables (numeric in IR72 crop file)
column5: cv. IR72 standard; function of development stage DVS
column6: ORYZA2000 (Bouman et al., 2001; Bouman & van Laar, 2006)
column9: DRIVES M_k(t). Structure: vegetative -> leaves+stems; DVS~0.65 (panicle initiation) -> stems; DVS>=1 (flowering) -> panicle (FSO->1), FRT->~0 by flowering. Extract numeric partitioning tables from the crop data file (IR72.D92).

column5: cv. IR72; sunlit chamber ~ field; 390 ppm CO2
column6: CO2 effects on rice water use & biomass, 2017
column9: M_total anchor. Strongly CO2-sensitive: 56% at 195 ppm, 128% at 780, 142% at 1560 ppm. Field yields ~5-10 t/ha grain.
Partitions biomass to grain (sink). Use ~0.5 for modern cultivars; cultivar-specific. Sets M_grain relative to aboveground biomass.

column6: Root-favoured biomass allocation in rice, 2018 (Acta Physiol. Plant.)
column9: Roots are a SMALL fraction of biomass -> M_root small vs shoot. Increases under drought; here paddy is flooded (low R/S).
Drives canopy light capture & the transpiration partition; FAO-56 gives Kcb-from-LAI relationship.
ETc = Kc x ET0. High Kc_ini reflects EVAPORATION from standing water (not transpiration) - see transpiration-split note below.
Field-measured Kc; close to FAO-56. Peak Kc at crop-development/reproductive.

column3: 6.61 mm/day (11 weeks after transplanting, reproductive; LAI 3.4)
column9: Sets the Q_TP(t) peak. Seasonal ET large due to ponded water + transpiration. Use TRANSPIRATION component for xylem advection (see note).
Compute ET0 from weather; ETc = Kc x ET0; transpiration T = ETc - Es feeds Q_TP(t).
Sets the time axis for M_k(t) and Q_TP(t). Use local cultivar duration (e.g. Korean japonica).

column3: simulate (phenology, LAI growth, DM distribution, transpiration)
column9: BEST ROUTE: run a calibrated crop model to GENERATE continuous M_k(t) and Q_TP(t) for your cultivar/climate, rather than tabulating. The values above are anchors/sanity checks.

column2: ORYZA2000 / ORYZA(v3) (Bouman et al. 2001; Bouman & van Laar 2006)
column3: DVS-based biomass partitioning, LAI growth, dry-matter distribution, daily transpiration - the core forcing generator.
Measured Kc (1.15/1.23/1.14/1.02) and peak ETc 6.61 mm/day at LAI 3.4.

column2: CO2 effects on rice water use & biomass, 2017
column3: WAGT ~17.4 t/ha (IR72) + CO2 sensitivity of biomass and Kc.
Rice root:shoot range and its drought/sink dependence.
Alternative process-based rice model (phenology, growth, water balance).

column2: C2 functions do not depend on PFAS - generate them ONCE from a calibrated crop model and reuse across all PFAS species.

In flooded paddy a LARGE share of ETc is evaporation from ponded water, NOT transpiration. Only the TRANSPIRATION component (T = ETc - Es, or the crop-model transpiration output) drives xylem advection of PFAS. Using total ETc would OVERESTIMATE Q_TP and thus upward PFAS transport.
Partitioning tables, HI, LAI, and duration are cultivar-dependent. Use Korean japonica parameters (or your study cultivar) rather than the IR72 defaults for a Korean paddy system.
Crop transpiration provides the root water-uptake sink for HYDRUS-1D; keep the water-balance consistent between the crop model and HYDRUS.
